company: Resonance Health Analysis Services Pty Ltd
sensitivity: 100.0% (at 4.1% VLFF threshold); 98.8% (at 12.1% VLFF threshold); 100.0% (at 16.2% VLFF threshold)
specificity: 98.6% (at 4.1% VLFF threshold); 98.0% (at 12.1% VLFF threshold); 99.6% (at 16.2% VLFF threshold)
intended_use: HepaFatSmart is intended for the quantitative measurement of volumetric liver fat fraction (VLFF), proton density fat fraction (PDFF) and steatosis grading. HepaFatSmart is an application that is used
intended_use_full: HepaFatSmart is intended for the quantitative measurement of volumetric liver fat fraction (VLFF), proton density fat fraction (PDFF) and steatosis grading. HepaFatSmart is an application that is used for the non-invasive evaluation of liver tissue by utilising magnetic resonance images to evaluate the difference in resonance frequencies between hydrogen nuclei in water and triglyceride fat.
target_condition: Fatty liver disease (NAFLD), metabolic syndrome, liver steatosis
ground_truth: HepaFat-Scan (predicate device with manual region of interest selection)
other_metrics: Bland-Altman analysis bias of 0.2% (95% CI 0.1 to 0.3); Upper 95% limit of agreement 1.7% (95% CI 1.5 to 1.8); Lower 95% limit of agreement -1.3% (95% CI -1.1 to -1.4); 100% reproducibility in repeatability study
